methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Intuitive Machines is a SPAC-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $305M to date. The valuation exercise operates within a competitive peer universe of 5 public comps (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX and Blue Origin, though the subset of directly comparable private companies at similar development stages remains sparse.

Standard triangulation methods face material constraints. Peer-median analysis is not applicable: while 23 same-sector peers exist across Space & Aerospace, the target does not fall within a sufficiently dense peer cluster when filtered by stage or valuation tier—only 2 same-stage companies were identified. Public-comp anchoring via multiples is similarly inapplicable because Intuitive Machines' revenue is not on file, which is typical for private companies. The sector median P/S multiple of 59.8x across the 6 public tickers cannot be deployed without the target's revenue figure.

The absence of applicable quantitative methods leaves valuation largely dependent on qualitative stage assessment and capital-intensity benchmarks within the sector. The $305M in total capital raised serves as a floor for analysis, but no range can be derived from the available data without either (1) disclosure of the target's revenue or near-term revenue projections, or (2) expansion of the private peer set to enable meaningful comparable-company clustering. Additional diligence on unit economics, customer concentration, and path to profitability is required to support a defensible valuation.
